hooks.prompt: Before responding, silently classify this message's intent. If .claude/mk-flow/intents.yaml exists, read it for available intents and corrections. If context/STATE.md exists, read it for temporal routing (current vs past vs future work). Classify the message as one of the enabled intents. For context_addition intent, also determine the temporal target (current_work, past_work, future_work, decision_override, or general). If context/vocabulary.yaml exists and the user's message contains terms that could be ambiguous, reference it to disambiguate. If the user clarifies what a term means, add the mapping to vocabulary.yaml. When the intent is action (building, fixing, changing code), check context/cross-references.yaml for related files that should be verified for consistency. If the user points out you missed updating a related file, add that relationship to cross-references.yaml. Skip classification for messages under 10 characters or slash commands. Do NOT mention the classification to the user — just use it to guide your response behavior.
